text_a: When he got back to Nuremberg in 1495, Durer started his own shop (you had to be married to do this). Over the next five years, his style started mixing Italian influences with Northern styles. Durer's daddy passed away in 1502, and his mama in 1513. His best pieces in the early years of the shop was his woodcut prints, mostly about religion, but he also did stuff like The Men's Bath House (around 1496). These were bigger and cut finer than most German woodcuts before, and they were way more complex and balanced. People now think it's unlikely that Durer cut any of the woodblocks himself; a specialist probably did that job. But, his training in Wolgemut's studio, where they made a lot of carved and painted altarpieces and designed and cut woodblocks for woodcuts, clearly taught him a lot about what you could do with the technique and how to work with block cutters. Durer either drew his design straight on the woodblock, or glued a paper drawing to the block. Either way, his drawings got destroyed when the block was cut.
text_b: How long after he opened his workshop did Durer make The Men's Bath House? Some months later

text_a: Taliban and American aid. After the September 11 attacks, it was necessary for conservatives to somehow explain away the fact that the US government gave 245 million dollars to the new evildoers du jour. Never mind that this is all documented on Michael Moore's website about the film. Gun homicides. text_a: When he got back to Nuremberg in 1495, Durer opened his own shop (gotta be married to do that). His top works in them early years of the shop were his woodcut prints, mostly 'bout religion, but he had some secular stuff too like The Men's Bath House (round 1496). These were bigger and cut finer than most German woodcuts before, and they were way more complex and balanced in composition. Folks now think it's unlikely that Durer cut any of them woodblocks himself; a specialist craftsman probably did that. But his training in Wolgemut's studio, where they made a lot of carved and painted altarpieces and both designed and cut woodblocks for woodcut, for sure gave him a deep understanding of what you could do with the technique, and how to work with them block cutters. Durer either drew his design straight on the woodblock, or glued a paper drawing to the block. Either way, his drawings got destroyed when they cut the block.
text_b: How long after he opened his workshop did Durer's mama pass away? Durer's mama passed away 18 years after he started his workshop.

text_a: When he got back to Nuremberg in 1495, Durer started up his own spot (had to be married to do that). Over the next five years, his style started mixing in Italian vibes with them Northern roots. Durer's pops passed in 1502, and his mama in 1513. The best stuff he did early on in the shop was his woodcut prints, mostly about religion, but he threw in some everyday scenes too, like The Men's Bath House (around 1496). These cuts were bigger and sharper than most German woodcuts before, and they were way more detailed and balanced. Most folks think Durer ain’t really cut the woodblocks himself; that was likely done by someone who specialized in that. But his time training in Wolgemut's studio, where they made a lot of carved and painted altarpieces and designed and cut woodblocks for prints, definitely taught him a lot about the craft and how to work with the folks who cut the blocks. Durer either sketched his design straight on the woodblock, or he stuck a paper drawing on it. Either way, his drawings got ruined when they cut the block.
text_b: How long after he opened his shop did Durer's pops pass away? 6
